symptoms.symptom_name: Retrosternal burning with sour acid regurgitation, worse recumbent/post-prandial

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acid regurgitation with postural/meal-triggered burning is characteristic of GORD.
Reflux-type features do not exclude ACS, which can present atypically.

symptoms.symptom_name: No exertional trigger / no arm-jaw radiation / no diaphoresis / no dyspnoea

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of exertional pattern, radiation, diaphoresis and dyspnoea lowers ACS probability but does not exclude it without ECG/troponin.
Partial antacid relief is consistent with acid-related pain but is not diagnostic and does not exclude ACS.

symptoms.symptom_name: Nocturnal cough and morning hoarseness (extra-oesophageal reflux)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic nocturnal cough and morning hoarseness are recognised extra-oesophageal manifestations of reflux.
Absence of bleeding/dysphagia/weight loss lowers the probability of complicated peptic disease/malignancy.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: 12-lead ECG (in person/ED) if any cardiac doubt
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Required to help exclude ACS in acute chest pain; cannot be done via chat — arrange in person / ED if any cardiac feature or significant risk.
Consider as part of GORD/dyspepsia work-up in follow-up.
For alarm features or refractory symptoms — not acutely.
